quiz.title: Algebra 2: Unit 10 Quiz 2
quiz.subtitle: 10.2: Normal Distributions and the Empirical Rule
quiz.subject: Mathematics
quiz.topic: Understanding the properties of the bell curve and the 68-95-99.7 rule

questions.question: A normal distribution has $\mu = 60$ and $\sigma = 8$. Within what range does 68% of the data fall?
The weights of newborn babies at a hospital are normally distributed with a mean of $7.5$ pounds and a standard deviation of $0.5$ pounds. What range of weights contains 95% of the newborns?
A set of data points has a mean of 150 and a standard deviation of 20. If the distribution is normal, what is the probability that a value is greater than 190?
questions.explanation: 190 = $150 + 2(20) = \mu + 2\sigma$. 95% is within $\pm 2\sigma$, so 5% is outside. Half of that (2.5%) is in the upper tail.

questions.question: If a normal distribution has a mean of $50$ and $99.7\%$ of the data is between $20$ and $80$, what is the standard deviation ($\sigma$)?
questions.explanation: 99.7% is $\mu \pm 3\sigma$. Distance from mean to edge is $80 - 50 = 30 = 3\sigma$, so $\sigma = 10$.

questions.question: A normal distribution has $\mu = 100$ and $\sigma = 15$. What percentage of data falls between 85 and 130?
questions.explanation: 85 = $\mu - 1\sigma$, 130 = $\mu + 2\sigma$. From $-1\sigma$ to mean = 34%. Mean to $+2\sigma$ = 47.5%. Sum = 81.5%.

questions.question: In a normal distribution with mean $\mu$ and standard deviation $\sigma$, what percentage of the data lies between $\mu$ and $\mu + 1\sigma$?
questions.explanation: 68% lies within $\pm 1\sigma$ on both sides. By symmetry, half of that (34%) lies between the mean and $+1\sigma$.

questions.question: A test score distribution is normal with $\mu = 75$ and $\sigma = 10$. Approximately what percentage of scores fall between $65$ and $85$?
questions.explanation: $65 = \mu - 1\sigma$, $85 = \mu + 1\sigma$. By the Empirical Rule, this is 68%.

questions.question: A set of measurements has $\mu = 1{,}200$ and $\sigma = 100$. What percentage of the data falls between $1{,}100$ and $1{,}400$?
questions.explanation: $1{,}100 = \mu - 1\sigma$, $1{,}400 = \mu + 2\sigma$. From $-1\sigma$ to mean = 34%. Mean to $+2\sigma$ = 47.5%. Sum = 81.5%.

questions.question: A machine fills containers with a mean of $20$ oz and a standard deviation of $0.05$ oz. If the distribution is normal, what percentage of containers have less than $19.95$ oz?
questions.explanation: $19.95 = \mu - 1\sigma$. 68% is within $\pm 1\sigma$, so 32% is outside. Half of that (16%) is in the lower tail.

questions.question: A normal distribution has $\mu = 200$ and $\sigma = 25$. What value corresponds to the 97.5th percentile?
questions.explanation: 97.5% is below $\mu + 2\sigma$ (since 2.5% is in the upper tail). $200 + 2(25) = 250$.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered the Empirical Rule — finding ranges for 68-95-99.7%, calculating tail probabilities, solving for $\sigma$, and working with asymmetric intervals.
scoreMessages.70: Good job. Key reminders: (1) 68% = $\mu \pm 1\sigma$, 95% = $\mu \pm 2\sigma$, 99.7% = $\mu \pm 3\sigma$, (2) For tail probabilities, halve the outside percentage, (3) For asymmetric intervals, add the piece percentages from the mean to each boundary.
scoreMessages.50: Keep practicing. Focus on: (1) Drawing the bell curve and labeling $\mu$, $\mu\pm\sigma$, $\mu\pm2\sigma$, $\mu\pm3\sigma$, (2) Half of 68% (34%) is on each side of the mean, (3) The right tail above $+2\sigma$ is 2.5%, (4) Percentiles relate to how many standard deviations from the mean.
scoreMessages.0: Review the Empirical Rule: (1) In a normal distribution: 68% within $\mu \pm 1\sigma$, 95% within $\mu \pm 2\sigma$, 99.7% within $\mu \pm 3\sigma$, (2) The curve is symmetric — 50% on each side of the mean, (3) Tail probabilities: $(100 - \text{middle}\%) / 2$ in each tail, (4) To find $\sigma$, divide the interval half-width by the number of standard deviations.
